findings.message: New runtime service `widgetizer` is defined only in `infra/compose/dev.yml`, a dev-scoped overlay. `dev` composes base+observability+dev and `dev:lite` composes base+dev, so this service reaches local dev only. Staging/prod compose the self-contained `<env>.yml` + `services.yml` + `observability.yml` (per infra/deploy/README.md + strategy.sh) — none of which include `dev.yml` — so `widgetizer` will silently NOT be deployed to staging/prod. It is not local-developer-experience tooling (it runs a published application image `ghcr.io/acme/widgetizer:1.4.0` and depends on the app `postgres`), so this is a service-parity gap, not an acknowledgeable dev convenience.
findings.suggestion: Define `widgetizer` in `infra/compose/services.yml` (which staging/prod compose alongside `<env>.yml` and `observability.yml`) so it reaches every deployed environment, or in the deployed `staging.yml`/`prod.yml` overlays directly — NOT `dev.yml` (and NOT `base.yml`, which is dev/test-only). If it is genuinely meant to be dev-only, record that decision in the decisions log rather than leaving it silently omitted from staging/prod.
